channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Meta’s summer sale drops Ray-Ban’s new smart glasses to record-low prices
item.description: If you’ve been curious about Ray-Ban’s Meta smart glasses, right now they’re on sale in Meta’s biggest deal event outside Black Friday. As part of the company’s Summer Sale event, which runs through May 26th, the original model is on sale starting at $224.25 ($74 off) at Amazon, Best Buy, and directly from Meta, matching […]

item.title: Procès OpenAI | Elon Musk absent, Sam Altman au premier rang
item.description: Trois semaines d’audiences à Oakland (Californie) et la trajectoire d’OpenAI en jeu : les créateurs de ChatGPT sont attentifs jeudi aux plaidoiries finales du procès intenté par Elon Musk, dernier acte avant que le jury ne se retire pour délibérer.

item.title: The VW ID.Buzz is returning for model year 2027 with a new trim for camping
item.description: The Tourer model features a fold-out mattress, removable window binds and more.

item.title: The HDMI ARC port on your TV has a very specific job
item.description: The distinction between various flavors of HDMI can get a bit technical.

item.title: Self-destructing $2,000 Nvidia chips will soon power tens of thousands of solar-run data centres hidden in lampposts
item.description: Under the new proposals, the lampposts in your street could double up as data centers with self-destructing, anti-tamper Nvidia chips.

item.title: OpenAI is reportedly preparing legal action against Apple; it wouldn’t be the first partner to feel burned
item.description: According to Bloomberg, OpenAI has enlisted an outside law firm to work through its options.

item.title: Clawdmeter turns your Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard
item.description: A new open source gadget called Clawdmeter turns Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard for AI coding power users.

item.title: Trump’s Tech Posse in China, Who’s Winning in Musk v. Altman, and Hantavirus Conspiracy Theories
item.description: Today on Uncanny Valley, we discuss how Donald Trump’s visit to China could influence conversations between world leaders at a moment when the economic and foreign policy stakes couldn’t be higher.

item.title: Microsoft starts canceling Claude Code licenses
item.description: Microsoft first started opening up access to Claude Code in December, inviting thousands of its own developers to use Anthropic's AI coding tool daily. It was part of an effort to get project managers, designers, and other employees to experiment with coding for the first time, and sources tell me that Claude Code has proved […]

item.title: What is the release date for Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 12 on CBS and Paramount+?
item.description: Kayce has just about survived an entire night on a freezing cold mountain. But now he's back, when does Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 12 hit CBS and Paramount+?

item.title: Early Memorial Day Tech Deals: Sony, Apple, Beats (2026)
item.description: Lots of our most-recommended headphones, power banks, and other gadgets are on sale for Memorial Day.

item.title: Linux devs are fighting the new age-gated internet
item.description: In January, Colorado lawmakers introduced a proposal to make operating systems collect users' ages and pass them to app developers. The bill, SB26-051, had clearly been designed for commercial platforms like iOS and Android - one of numerous plans to age-gate the internet through users' devices. It was intended to provide information that would let […]...

item.title: YouTube viewers watch 2 billion hours of Shorts on TVs each month
item.description: Short-form video is built for mobile, so it may seem counterintuitive, but it's true: YouTube Shorts are becoming quite popular on the big screen.

item.title: Lowe's Memorial Day sale starts now — 45% off patio furniture, appliances, grills, tools, flowers, and more
item.description: Lowe's Memorial Day sale is officially live, and I'm rounding up the best deals you need for your dream backyard, including up to 45% off patio furniture, grills, flowers, and more.

item.title: Young founders are reshaping leadership
item.description: Why the next generation is building with portfolios of ventures and impact. Leadership is no longer linear. Among the founders I meet, there’s a clear shift: Younger entrepreneurs are starting earlier, building faster, and often working across multiple ventures at once. More than half of Gen Z has a side hustle. Entrepreneurship is beginning to look less like a single trajectory and more like a portfolio. ...

item.title: Microsoft's unreleased cloud controller has been spotted in the wild and it's teeny
item.description: It reportedly uses Wi-Fi to directly connect to the Xbox Cloud Gaming service, for lower latency.

item.title: Hed: Crunch time? Take health advice from these 23 leaders
item.description: From creatine to pickleball, each leader figured out methods to preserve health during stressful periods. Stress is built into every leader’s work life. But sometimes it’s even more intense. Just as airlines say to put on your own oxygen mask before helping others, executives need to take care of their physical and mental health to effectively lead the team and the company....

item.title: Spotify and its hosting platforms are adopting Apple's tech for video podcasts
item.description: HTTP Live Streaming support will let Apple Podcasts play Spotify-hosted video podcasts.

item.title: Another major Linux security issue uncovered - new Fragnesia flaw allows attackers to run malicious code as root
item.description: The flaw is in the same family as Dirty Frag and allows privilege escalation at kernel level.

item.title: Noble Audio to launch FoKus Apollo Pro headset for $699
item.description: An extra $50 will get you even nicer materials on the already high-end headphones.

item.title: Claude Code's '/goals' separates the agent that works from the one that decides it's done
item.description: A code migration agent finishes its run, and the pipeline looks green. But several pieces were never compiled — and it took days to catch. That's not a model failure; that's an agent deciding it was done before it actually was.Many enterprises are now seeing that production AI agent pipelines fail not because of the models’ abilities but because the model behind the agent decides to stop. Several methods to prevent premature task exits are now available from LangChain, Google and OpenAI, though these often rely on separate evaluation systems. The newest method comes from Anthropic: /goals on C...

item.title: Retour sur la Lune : pourquoi la Nasa joue gros avec SpaceX et Blue Origin
item.description: Fin 2028, une Lune, deux atterrisseurs, une course contre la montre. Depuis que la Nasa a arrêté sa nouvelle feuille de route pour le programme Artemis, les entreprises SpaceX et Blue Origin sont engagées dans une compétition aussi serrée qu'inédite : tester et qualifier leurs atterrisseurs...

item.title: The Esports World Cup will reportedly move to Paris from Riyadh
item.description: Uncertainty in the Middle East is apparently forcing the location change.

item.title: DJI dévoile l’Osmo Pocket 4P à Cannes : la caméra à double objectif qui veut jouer dans la cour des pros
item.description: DJI a présenté l'Osmo Pocket 4P à Cannes. Au programme : plage dynamique cinéma, 10 bits D-Log2 et basse lumière améliorée. Prix encore inconnu.

item.title: Bill Lawrence unfiltered: How Scrubs, Ted Lasso, Shrinking, and Rooster became hit shows
item.description: Bill Lawrence, the showrunner behind Scrubs, Ted Lasso, and Shrinking, explains his creative process, from finding the emotional core of a story to surviving writer’s block, writing through a pandemic, and building shows that can make you laugh one second and gut-punch you the next. Bill Lawrence, the showrunner behind Scrubs, Ted Lasso, and Shrinking, explains his creative process, from finding the emotional core of a story to surviving writer’s block, writing through a pandemic, and building shows that can make you laugh one second and gut-punch you the next....

item.title: DJI frappe fort avec l’Osmo Pocket 4P, une caméra de vlog « Pro » aux caractéristiques inédites
item.description: Après sa caméra Osmo Pocket 4, DJI a profité du festival de Cannes pour présenter son nouveau modèle doté de deux objectifs, la DJI Osmo Pocket 4P.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy A57 vs Samsung Galaxy A56 : comment choisir le meilleur téléphone Galaxy A ?
item.description: Les Samsung Galaxy A sont de véritables best-sellers. Cette année, le A57 succède naturellement au A56, mais se paie aussi une belle augmentation de prix. Comment bien choisir son futur smartphone ?
